creator: vbasiccd@msn.com
text:
I found the error message discussed on Stack Overflow:
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/72032032/importerror-cannot-import-name-iterable-from-collections-in-python

Apparently, in Python 3.10, "Iterable" was moved from "collections" to "collections.abc".  The suggestions on Stack Overflow was to simply rename "collections" to "collections.abc" on the import line in the Python script.

As a test, I edited /usr/local/lib/python3.11/site-packages/orangewidget/report/report.py and replaced the line:

from collections import OrderedDict, Iterable

with

from collections import OrderedDict
from collections.abc import Iterable

This allowed Orange3 to run.  However, it might be mute.  After the app loaded, it displayed a message about updating to version 3.37.  That's probably the more prudent move.
